trivia_bullets.text: Fires of Invention is a rare red enchantment from ELD (October 2019). Its smith-at-the-forge art evokes Embereth's Irencrag — the volcanic boulder central to Embereth's knighting ritual — though the card is not explicitly tied to the Irencrag in oracle text.
"Just tell us how many you want and get out of the way." — No named speaker on the card.

Banned in Standard and Historic on June 1, 2020 (~8 months post-release). Wizards cited a 55% win rate and favorable matchups against all top-ten archetypes. Determined to be a persistent design constraint: the free-spell engine would strengthen with every new high-cost card printed.
sources: Wizards of the Coast ban announcement, magic.wizards.com/en/news/announcements/june-1-2020-banned-and-restricted-announcement

trivia_bullets.text: Remains banned in Standard and Historic as of 2026; legal in Pioneer and Modern.
Hipsters of the Coast covered the ban in June 2020, grouping it with the companion-mechanic errata as part of a broader Throne of Eldraine design reckoning.
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: 'Agent of Treachery, Fires of Invention Banned in Standard and Historic, Companion Mechanic Errata'd'

related_cards.name: Agent of Treachery
related_cards.note: Banned simultaneously; its free cast via Fires was the dominant abuse vector

related_cards.name: Lukka, Coppercoat Outcast
related_cards.note: Companion whose mechanic was errata'd at the same June 2020 announcement

related_cards.name: Irencrag Feat
related_cards.note: Thematic sibling; both cards reference Irencrag as a source of explosive red magic
